message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: TKB Bakery & Deli (Indio)
Cuisine/type signal: Sandwich shop / bakery
Why it surfaced in discovery: Named #1 Restaurant in America on Yelp's Top 100 in 2018, repeat Top-5 finisher; family-run (the 'Sippels'), origin as door-to-door cookie dough business; LA Taco-cited destination; pulled into festival-adjacent culture (TKB has appeared inside Coachella festival grounds).
Discovery sources: https://www.tkbbakery.com/ | https://nbcpalmsprings.com/2018/02/21/tkb-bakery-and-deli-in-indio-named-yelps-best/ | https://lataco.com/best-restaurants-coachella-indio

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's information about TKB Bakery & Deli in Indio reviews from 2025-2026:

## Recent Reviews (2025-2026)

TKB Bakery & Deli has been serving the community for over 15 years, with the deli located inside their factory in Indio.

### Positive Feedback

A September 2025 review praised the massive, flavorful sandwiches made with fresh, high-quality ingredients. The staff delivers friendly, attentive service, and the restaurant features unique breads baked on-site and a wide selection of bakery treats.

For 2026, local road construction may affect access to the restaurant, and another recent review noted the Trump Card sandwich was "dynamite" and the focaccia bread was delicious.

### Overall Ratings

The restaurant has a 4.7 Google rating, and it's ranked #1 of 132 restaurants in Indio on Tripadvisor. Additionally, it was named the #1 Restaurant in America in 2018 by Yelp.

### Some Concerns

A few reviews mentioned concerns, such as a mandatory 5% service charge added to offset costs, and some customers reported that recent visits were disappointing compared to previous experiences at older locations.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about TKB Bakery, the Sippel family, and their sandwich shop:

## Business Origins and History

TKB Bakery & Deli was started in 1991 when 5-year-old Melina, 7-year-old Nathan, and 10-year-old Brandon began selling cookie dough door to door made by their mother, Athena, who worked as a pastry chef at The Vintage Club in Indian Wells. Their Rancho Mirage neighbors couldn't get enough of the cookie dough, and the family got involved with school fundraisers and started selling to local businesses. Athena rented kitchen space at Mission Hills Country Club, and the kids earned their allowances by cracking eggs and mixing dough.

## Growth and Evolution

Retail sales were weak, so they rejiggered the package size and began selling 10-pound "scoop-and-bake" tubs of dough wholesale to grocery stores. The plan worked, and the Sippels landed larger accounts including Gelson's stores in Los Angeles and Whole Foods locations throughout Arizona.

When a church renting space next door in their industrial park moved out in 2001, Paul got the idea to make breads, hire a deli manager, and feed the industrial park. TKB stands for "The Kids Business".

## Recent Success

TKB was named the No. 1 restaurant in America on Yelp in 2018, and business practically quadrupled overnight.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the best sandwich shop in the Coachella Valley and Indio area:

## TKB Bakery & Deli

TKB Bakery & Deli is located at 45334 Golf Center Pkwy in Indio, California and stands out as the top-rated sandwich shop in the region.

**Recognition and Awards:**
TKB is so well-regarded that Yelp keeps naming them to their best in the nation list. The sandwich spot was named the No. 1 restaurant in America on Yelp in 2018.

**What They Offer:**
The Indio hotspot is well known for their menu which features incredible, piled-high sandwiches plus a bunch of tasty bakery treats. Their #1 Top Seller is called "The Trump," which they claim is the Best Sandwich in the Coachella Valley.

**Quality:**
The breads are fresh daily and protein and veggies were top notch. The Sippels started the business in 1991, selling their mother's cookie dough door to door before expanding their offerings.

**Customer Experience:**
TKB is ranked #1 of 17 Quick Bites in Indio on TripAdvisor, and they offer complimentary cookies with sandwich purchases.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about TKB Bakery and its Yelp credentials and awards:

## Yelp Top 100 Rankings

TKB Bakery & Deli was the number one business on the 2018 Yelp Top 100 Restaurants List, and placed in the top five in 2015, 2016 and 2017. More specifically, TKB was ranked number 1 in 2018, #5 in 2017 and 2016, and #4 in 2015. In 2019, the Coachella Valley sandwich joint came in at #37.

## About TKB Bakery

The Sippels own TKB Bakery & Deli in Indio, CA, and TKB stands for "The Kids Business" because that is exactly what it is. The business started with Melina and her two brothers, Brandon and Nathan, selling mom's cookie dough door-to-door as kids.

## Business Excellence

The Indio, California bakery was able to receive so many positive reviews thanks to its superb sandwiches and baked goods, as well as its commitment to customer service.

## Top Recommendations:

**Serrano's Juice Bar & Grill** - Serrano's Juice Bar & Grill is open until 3:00 every day serving customizable fresh juices from a long list of produce to pair with their homemade breakfasts. These establishments offer a variety of breakfast and lunch options with quality ingredients and hearty portions.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2026:

## Fine Dining Options

The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine. Le Vallauris in Palm Springs is a top choice for classic French cuisine in a romantic setting.

Pacifica Seafood Restaurant in Palm Desert is known for its fresh catches and extensive wine list, with a menu including oysters to Chilean sea bass.

Located at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage, The Edge Steakhouse specializes in prime steaks and fresh seafood with top-notch service and a luxurious setting.

Copley's On Palm Canyon is housed in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate and offers a mix of American and European cuisine with seasonal ingredients, featuring al fresco dining in the courtyard.

## Other Notable Restaurants

Top restaurants in Coachella include Red Falcon, El Tranvia Restaurant, Papa Headz, Kitchen Ten Eleven, The Coachella Firehouse Bar And Grill, and Indio Taphouse.

JT's Diner in Palm Desert offers classic American dining with hearty meals and nostalgic charm.

Jackalope Ranch in Indio is known for its rustic and lively atmosphere with a diverse menu featuring Southwestern and American cuisine.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about TKB Bakery at Coachella and Stagecoach 2024:

**Location & Setup:**
TKB Bakery and Deli can be found on the Coachella/Stagecoach grounds near Spectra (the colorful tower), right next to the Pies & Lows restaurant.

**First Festival Appearance:**
TKB Bakery and Deli is at its first Coachella this year. You can find Everbloom Coffee and TKB Bakery on the Empire Polo Club grounds for both weekends of Coachella, and the single weekend for Stagecoach.

**About the Restaurant:**
TKB was No. 1 on the Yelp Top 100 Places To Eat List in 2018—the fourth year in a row TKB finished in Yelp's Top 5. Everything's made fresh every day from scratch.

**Festival Experience:**
According to one of the co-owners, Melina Sippel, all the bread was baked fresh that morning, and it's just five miles away from the festival grounds, highlighting the local nature of the business.

Based on the search results, here's information about these three Coachella Valley sandwich competitors:

## Sherman's Deli & Bakery

Since opening its first location in 1963, Sherman's has become a landmark in the desert and is a New York-style family restaurant still owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son, Sam and daughter Janet. The deli has two locations in Palm Springs and Palm Desert. Their Famous Sandwich Board offers more than 40 favorites including Hot Corned Beef piled high on rye bread served with Potato Salad or Cole Slaw.

## Manhattan in the Desert

Founded by Sherman Harris in 1953, this spot is a Palm Springs institution, with everyone from Frank Sinatra to Bob Hope to Rita Hayworth popping by in its early days. Manhattan in the Desert is a genuine New York-style Jewish deli specializing in lox, homemade soups, hot dogs and sandwiches, the latter of which are all served "mile-high," stacked to perfection. Its signature Manhattan-size sandwich is made with corned beef, pastrami, turkey, roast beef, Swiss cheese, and tomato and served on rye bread.

## Aspen Mills

Originally established in 1995, this spot is best known for its supreme bread offerings with more than 30 varieties to choose from (including gluten-free options), all handmade and stone ground on the premises each day. With additional locations in Palm Springs and Rancho Mirage, Aspen Mills is a true desert stalwart.

content.text: Site is built on Square Online (Weebly platform) — JSON-LD restaurant schema is unlikely but I confirmed OpenGraph meta tags. Let me check for JSON-LD specifically.

input.description: Count JSON-LD blocks and itemtype attributes

content.text: No JSON-LD or microdata — only OpenGraph meta tags. Site is built on Square Online. Address confirmed at 45334 Golf Center Pkwy, Indio. Now I have enough.

input.name: TKB Bakery & Deli

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 4,900+ reviews; Tripadvisor ~540 reviews; Google rating reported 4.7 (Google review count [UNVERIFIED] — could not retrieve count directly)
reputation.longevity: Founded 1991 as a kids' cookie-dough business by the Sippel family; deli operation launched 2001; still family-owned and operated (Athena, Paul, and children Melina, Brandon, Nathan Sippel); new building opened in Indio in 2022
credentials: Yelp Top 100 Places to Eat in the U.S. — #1 in 2018
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice; ranked #1 of 17 Quick Bites in Indio
Featured in Palm Springs Life ('The Sandwich Generation')
Featured in LA Taco best-of Coachella/Indio guide
Selected as on-grounds vendor at Coachella Valley Music & Arts Festival and Stagecoach (2024)
Visit Greater Palm Springs feature ('All in the Family')
KESQ / NBC Palm Springs local coverage
reputation.recency_signal: Recent (2025–2026) reviews consistently praise sandwich quality, fresh-baked bread (jalapeño focaccia frequently called out), and generous portions. Common friction points: long lines during peak season, a mandatory ~5% service charge, occasional service inconsistency, and 2026 road-construction access issues around Golf Center Pkwy. Sentiment direction: stable-positive, not declining.

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best sandwich Coachella Valley', 'best sandwich Indio', 'best lunch Indio', 'Coachella festival food', 'Yelp top restaurants Indio'. Does NOT surface for broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'best breakfast Indio' lists in agent answers (those default to Sloan's, Keedy's, Jackalope Ranch, Purple Room, Le Vallauris, etc.) — TKB is siloed in sandwich/deli vertical despite higher national press than most of those.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When surfaced, descriptions are accurate: family-owned, Indio sandwich shop, Yelp 2018 #1, scratch-baked bread. Origin story (cookie dough door-to-door, 'The Kids Business' acronym) is consistently correct in agent responses.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: tkbbakery.com is built on Square Online (Weebly). Site carries OpenGraph meta tags (og:title, og:description, og:url, og:site_name, og:type=website) and a vertical='quick-serve-restaurant' Square identifier — but NO schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness blocks and NO microdata itemtype markup. Hours, address (45334 Golf Center Pkwy, Indio), and menu live in Square's CMS but are not exposed as machine-readable Restaurant/Menu schema. Square's hosted ordering provides menu structure for Square's own ecosystem but not for general agent crawlers.

competitors: Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Desert / Palm Springs)
Aspen Mills Bakery (Palm Desert / Palm Springs / Rancho Mirage)
Keedy's Indio (adjacent breakfast/lunch counter in same city)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor (festival-adjacent Indio) — TKB is the highest-national-profile independent food business in Indio proper and one of the few Coachella Valley restaurants with a credible national credential (Yelp #1 2018). It anchors the sandwich/deli category valley-wide, not just Indio.
query_wins: where to eat near Empire Polo Club

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Real owned website on Square Online (paid platform, custom domain), active Facebook page, e-commerce / online ordering via Square, presence on DoorDash and Grubhub (paid marketplace fees), Giftly gift card program, on-grounds Coachella/Stagecoach festival vendor slot (significant paid commitment), 2022 new-building capex. No agency footprint visible; marketing appears in-house and PR-driven (earned media via Yelp ranking).
convertibility.reasoning: TKB is a textbook convertible: independently owned by an identifiable, reachable local family (the Sippels), with multi-decade operating history, a 2022 capital investment in a new Indio facility, active multi-channel marketing spend (Square site, social, delivery marketplaces, festival vendor fees), and a major reputation asset (Yelp #1) that is currently underleveraged for agent discovery because the website lacks schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness/Menu JSON-LD. The gap between reputation (national press, 4.7 across platforms, ~5,000 Yelp reviews) and structured agent-readability (zero JSON-LD, OpenGraph only) is exactly the conversion wedge.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Exact Google Business Profile review count — Google rating of 4.7 is widely cited but the underlying count was not directly retrieved (Yelp blocked WebFetch with 403; Google not directly queryable here).
[UNVERIFIED] Whether TKB returned as on-grounds Coachella/Stagecoach vendor in 2025 or 2026 — confirmed only for 2024.

[UNVERIFIED] Current operating hours — site relies on Square CMS that did not render in WebFetch; address (45334 Golf Center Pkwy) and phone presence on Yelp listing confirmed via search snippets but not directly read.
[UNVERIFIED] Claim that 'business practically quadrupled overnight' after 2018 Yelp #1 — sourced from Yelp's own blog (self-reported, not independently audited).
